title: Modelling
text: Creating model:
code: mod <- lr_model(formula = ae1 ~ aucss, data = lr_data) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4

title: Modelling
text: lr_predict() function produces model predictions: confidence level can adjusted using conf_level argument

title: Modelling
text: two functions control SCM regression, lr_scm_forward() lr_scm_backward(): extract log, use lr_scm_history():
code: base_mod <- lr_model(formula = ae1 ~ aucss, data = lr_data) candidates <- c("sex", "dose", "weight", "age")  final_mod <- base_mod |>    lr_scm_forward(candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = 3425) |>    lr_scm_backward(candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = 9821)  final_mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4 lr_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> 2         1       1 forward    add    ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 forward    add    ~weight     ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         1       3 forward    add    ~age        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 5         1       4 forward    add    ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

title: Plotting
text: Basic usage  Adding extra components
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(exposure = aucss, response = ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() lr_data |>    lr_plot(exposure = aucss, response = ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>   lr_plot_show_datastrip() |>   lr_plot_show_groups(group_by = aucss) |>    plot()

title: Plotting
text: Stratification adds colour across components  can suppress stratification specific components
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    lr_plot_show_datastrip() |>   plot() lr_data |>    lr_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    lr_plot_show_model(keep_strata = FALSE) |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    lr_plot_show_datastrip() |>   plot()

title: Plotting
text: Default style = "ribbonline" can also draw spaghetti plots represent parameter uncertainty
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model(style = "spaghetti") |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() #> Using seed = 4371 #> Warning in ggplot2::geom_path(data = sim, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = #> .data[[exposure$name]], : Ignoring unknown parameters: `fill`

title: Plotting
text: can modify number bins:  can also modify confidence level Clopper-Pearson interval:
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 6) |>    plot() lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 6, conf_level = .8) |>    plot()

title: Plotting
text: Multiple grouping variables allowed:  Stratification propagates group component:  default style = "boxplot" can also use violin plots:
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>   lr_plot_show_groups(group_by = c(aucss, sex)) |>    plot() lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>   lr_plot_show_groups(group_by = aucss) |>    plot() lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>   lr_plot_show_groups(group_by = sex, style = "violin") |>    plot()

text: Navarro D (2026). erlr: Exposure-Response Tools Logistic Regression Models. R package version 0.0.9.9000, https://github.com/djnavarro/erlr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {erlr: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models},   author = {Danielle Navarro},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.9.9000},   url = {https://github.com/djnavarro/erlr}, }

what: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
text: Provides estimation plotting tools exposure-response models use logistic regression binary responses. mostly intended convenience package: core tools wrappers around glm(), plotting tools use ggplot2 patchwork build typical plots used exposure-response modelling.

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
text: can install development version erlr like :
code: pak::pak("djnavarro/erlr")

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: library(erlr) library(tibble)  lr_data #> # A tibble: 300 × 10 #>       id sex      age weight  dose treatment aucss cmaxss   ae1   ae2 #>    <int> <fct>  <int>  <dbl> <dbl> <fct>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1     1 Male      35     79   200 Drug       673.   97.3     0     1 #>  2     2 Female    22     58   200 Drug      2806.  301.      1     1 #>  3     3 Female    28     58     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 #>  4     4 Female    18     57   100 Drug      1169.  198.      1     1 #>  5     5 Male      28     77   100 Drug       377.   51.4     0     0 #>  6     6 Female    19     76   200 Drug       327.   25.4     1     0 #>  7     7 Male      30     70     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 #>  8     8 Female    34     60   100 Drug      1208.  133.      1     1 #>  9     9 Male      21     89     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 #> 10    10 Female    34     56   200 Drug       254.   31.0     0     0 #> # ℹ 290 more rows  mod <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss, lr_data) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    lr_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>    plot() plt <- lr_data |>     lr_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>     lr_plot_show_model(keep_strata = FALSE) |>     lr_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 3) |>     lr_plot_show_datastrip() |>     lr_plot_show_groups(group_by = c(aucss, treatment), keep_strata = FALSE)  print(plt) #> <erlr_plot> #>   plot variables: #>     - exposure:        aucss #>     - response:        ae2 #>     - stratification:  sex #>   plot components: #>     - model:           ae2 ~ aucss #>     - quantile:        3 bins #>     - strip:           jitter both #>     - group:           .aucss_quantile, treatment #>   plots built: <none> #>   output built: no plot(plt)

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: mod1 <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex + dose, lr_data) mod2 <- lr_scm_backward(mod1, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 7932 lr_scm_history(mod2) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model <NA>   <NA>        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 2         1       1 backward   remove ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         2       3 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: mod <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, lr_data) sim <- lr_vpc_sim(mod, seed = 1234) sim #> # A tibble: 30,000 × 5 #>      ae1 aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>    <dbl> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1 0.894  673. Male        1      1 #>  2 1.00  2806. Female      2      1 #>  3 0.110    0  Female      3      1 #>  4 0.993 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5 0.588  377. Male        5      1 #>  6 0.468  327. Female      6      1 #>  7 0.129    0  Male        7      1 #>  8 0.994 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9 0.129    0  Male        9      1 #> 10 0.362  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 29,990 more rows  lr_vpc_plot(mod, sim, group_by = aucss) lr_vpc_plot(mod, sim, group_by = sex)

what: Partial builders for logistic regression plots — build_datastrip_jitter
title: Partial builders for logistic regression plots — build_datastrip_jitter
text: Partial builders logistic regression plots

title: Partial builders for logistic regression plots — build_datastrip_jitter
code: build_datastrip_jitter(   data,   config,   stratify,   exposure,   response,   strata,   style )  build_group_boxplot(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style)  build_group_violin(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style)  build_model_ribbonline(   data,   config,   stratify,   exposure,   response,   strata,   style )  build_model_spaghetti(   data,   config,   stratify,   exposure,   response,   strata,   style )  build_quantile_errorbar(   data,   config,   stratify,   exposure,   response,   strata,   style )  build_summary_pvalue(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style)

title: Partial builders for logistic regression plots — build_datastrip_jitter
text: data original data frame config Configuration specific plot stratify Logical indicating whether stratify exposure Exposure variable response Response variable strata Stratification variable style Style components

title: Partial builders for logistic regression plots — build_datastrip_jitter
text: geom, list geoms. precisely, list objects can added ggplot2 plot. expectation objects added partially-constructed plot , minimum, already base theme applied. "model", "summary", "quantile", pieces added plot already coord sets axis limits. "datastrip" "group" plots, plot object yet coord. expectation, however, builder supply x-axis limit consistent base plot. , since component plots use exposure variable x-axis, use values stored exposure$limits tp set x-axis limits.

title: Partial builders for logistic regression plots — build_datastrip_jitter
text: Things can partials : model summary quantile datastrip group Arguments standardised allow users write needed

what: Builds an exposure-response plot for a logistic regression model — lr_plot
title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a logistic regression model — lr_plot
text: Builds exposure-response plot logistic regression model

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a logistic regression model — lr_plot
code: lr_plot(data, exposure, response, stratify_by = NULL)  lr_plot_style(object, labels)  lr_plot_show_model(   object,   keep_strata = NULL,   style = "ribbonline",   conf_level = 0.95 )  lr_plot_show_quantiles(   object,   keep_strata = NULL,   style = "errorbar",   bins = 4,   conf_level = 0.95 )  lr_plot_show_datastrip(   object,   keep_strata = NULL,   style = "jitter",   panel = "both" )  lr_plot_show_groups(   object,   group_by,   style = "boxplot",   bins = NULL,   keep_strata = NULL )  lr_plot_build(object)

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a logistic regression model — lr_plot
text: data Observed data exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted) stratify_by Stratification variable used color fill (one variable, unquoted) object Partially constructed plot (S3 class erlr_plot) labels Named list labels keep_strata Logical, indicating whether component keep color stratification style Character string used specify partial builder component conf_level Confidence level bins Number exposure bins (counting placebo) panel Character string: "upper", "lower", "" (default) group_by Grouping variables define groups distribution plots (tidyselection variables)

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a logistic regression model — lr_plot
text: Plot object class erlr_plot

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a logistic regression model — lr_plot
code: lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    lr_plot_show_model() |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    lr_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>    plot()    plt <- lr_data |>    lr_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>    lr_plot_show_model(keep_strata = FALSE) |>    lr_plot_show_quantiles() |>    lr_plot_show_datastrip() |>    lr_plot_show_groups(group_by = c(aucss, treatment), keep_strata = FALSE)  print(plt) #> <erlr_plot> #>   plot variables: #>     - exposure:        aucss #>     - response:        ae2 #>     - stratification:  sex #>   plot components: #>     - model:           ae2 ~ aucss #>     - quantile:        4 bins #>     - strip:           jitter both #>     - group:           .aucss_quantile, treatment #>   plots built: <none> #>   output built: no plot(plt)

title: Predictions and confidence intervals for logistic regression — lr_predict
code: mod <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss, lr_data) prd <- lr_predict(mod, lr_data) prd #> # A tibble: 300 × 15 #>       id sex      age weight  dose treatment aucss cmaxss   ae1   ae2 fit_link #>    <int> <fct>  <int>  <dbl> <dbl> <fct>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1     1 Male      35     79   200 Drug       673.   97.3     0     1  1.91    #>  2     2 Female    22     58   200 Drug      2806.  301.      1     1 13.6     #>  3     3 Female    28     58     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 -1.79    #>  4     4 Female    18     57   100 Drug      1169.  198.      1     1  4.64    #>  5     5 Male      28     77   100 Drug       377.   51.4     0     0  0.283   #>  6     6 Female    19     76   200 Drug       327.   25.4     1     0  0.00668 #>  7     7 Male      30     70     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 -1.79    #>  8     8 Female    34     60   100 Drug      1208.  133.      1     1  4.85    #>  9     9 Male      21     89     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 -1.79    #> 10    10 Female    34     56   200 Drug       254.   31.0     0     0 -0.397   #> # ℹ 290 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: se_link <dbl>, fit_resp <dbl>, ci_lower <dbl>, #> #   ci_upper <dbl>

what: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
text: Stepwise covariate modelling logistic regression

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
code: lr_scm_forward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = NULL)  lr_scm_backward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = NULL)  lr_scm_history(mod)

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
text: mod erlr model object candidates Character vector list candidate terms threshold Threshold test seed Optional seed control order term tests

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
text: lr_scm_forward() lr_scm_backward(), updated erlr model returned, SCM history log updated internally. lr_scm_history(), data frame returned containing SCM history log

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
code: mod0 <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss, lr_data) mod1 <- lr_scm_forward(mod0, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 2475 lr_scm_history(mod1) #> # A tibble: 3 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> 2         1       1 forward    add    ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 forward    add    ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>  mod2 <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex + dose, lr_data) mod3 <- lr_scm_backward(mod2, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 9165 lr_scm_history(mod3) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 2         1       1 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 backward   remove ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         2       3 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

what: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: Simulate logistic regression model

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
code: lr_simulator(object)

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: object logistic regression model

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: function arguments param, data, type. param argument vector coefficients data argument data frame tibble type argument string indicating type prediction generate (defaults "response") Takes fitted glm object input returns function evaluate underlying structural model user-specified parameters data (e.g., VPCs counterfactual simulation scenarios). principle work glms generally, merely logistic regressions, tested except logistic regression models

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
code: mod1 <- lr_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, lr_data) par1 <- coef(mod1) mod1_sim <- lr_simulator(mod1)  # no counterfactuals p1 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = lr_data)  p2 <- unname(predict(mod1, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the data set lr_data2 <- lr_data[1:20, ] p3 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = lr_data2)  p4 <- unname(predict(mod1, newdata = lr_data2, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the parameters par2 <- par1 int1 <- par1["(Intercept)"] par2["(Intercept)"] <- 0 p5 <- mod1_sim(param = par2, data = lr_data)
